items.title: Introducing GPT-5.3-Codex | OpenAI
items.author: openai.com
items.snippet:
OpenAI has been actively releasing new models and updates in early 2026. On March 5, 2026, they launched three new GPT-5 family models: GPT-5.3 Instant (fast, everyday tasks), GPT-5.4 Thinking (complex professional workflows), and GPT-5.4 Pro (highest-capability option). Earlier in February 2026, OpenAI introduced GPT-5.3-Codex and GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark — the latter being their first model designed specifically for real-time coding assistance.

OpenAI also made adjustments to thinking time in Janu

items.title: Introducing GPT-5.4 | OpenAI
items.author: openai.com
items.snippet: OpenAI has been actively releasing new models in early 2026, with GPT-5.4 being the most recent flagship release as of March 2026. GPT-5.4 is positioned as OpenAI's most powerful enterprise-focused model, combining reasoning, coding, and agentic workflows with native computer-use capabilities and up to 1 million tokens of context. It is described as the most token-efficient reasoning model yet, using significantly fewer tokens to solve problems compared to prior versions, and introduces upfront

items.title: Changelog | OpenAI API
items.author: platform.openai.com
items.snippet: OpenAI has released several significant API updates in early 2026. On the model front, GPT-5.4 mini and GPT-5.4 nano were added to the Chat Completions and Responses API — mini targets high-volume workloads with GPT-5.4-class capabilities including tool search and computer use, while nano is optimized for speed and cost on simple tasks. The Sora video API was also expanded with reusable character references, longer generations (up to 20 seconds), 1080p output for sora-2-pro, video extensions, an

items.title: Deepening our collaboration with the U.S. Department of Energy | OpenAI
items.author: openai.com
items.snippet: OpenAI made several significant announcements in March 2026. On March 24, the company confirmed the discontinuation of Sora, its AI video platform — the Sora Team posted the news on X (Twitter), citing failure to gain meaningful user traction, along with ongoing intellectual property and deepfake ethical concerns. Earlier in the month, OpenAI launched GPT-5.4 on March 5, a model explicitly designed for complex, multi-step professional tasks with substantially improved performance over prior vers
